channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overgrown tree, 6th May
item.description:
Overgrown tree

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sudbury Crescent, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 Human Forest bikes dumped ouside 12 Wellington Gardens, 6th May
item.description:
This is becoming a hot spot for leaving HF bikes which have nothing to do with the residents of no 12. SHIFT THEM PLEASE.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Charlton Church Lane, Charlton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paving stones cracked and broken, 6th May
item.description:
Paving stones recently replaced but already are cracked and trip hazards. This is predominantly outside of 9 Dunblane Road but is also the case on other parts of the street. Only noticed this morning, but could cause real problems as there are many elderly residents on this street.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dunblane Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At Church lane end of Floyd Road, 6th May
item.description:
Wooden pallet near Biffa bins plus disgusting mess of torn open carrier bags spilling waste all over the pavement. This is a HOT SPOT so why is there no cctv to aid catching the filthy and antisocial people who dump their rubbish. There are no signs in Charlton of Greenwich Council's crack down on fly tippers so was this a pre-election gimmick? Verdict: could do a lot better if you tried.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Church Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Excessive Litter, 6th May
item.description:
Excessive Litter all over the grass area and parking bay
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Evering Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in Church Lane, 6th May
item.description:
Bin bags all round litter bin near Marsh and Parsons at junction with Wellington Gardens. Rubbish at end of Sundorne Road possibly includes old carpet in black plastic.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Church Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of rubbish flytipped, 6th May
item.description:
Bin bags of rubbish fly tipped by the side of the road. Since this photo, foxes have torn open the bin bags and there is now food scattered all over the road and pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marmadon Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture dumped, 6th May
item.description:
Could you please remove

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mapledene Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped broken furniture, 6th May
item.description:
The tenants of 31 charleston street Se171ng have been seen dumping broken cupboards on the opposite corner (Cotham st junction) during the night. This has been there for 4 days and is a hazard and eyesore

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charleston Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins left on the pavement after collection, 6th May
item.description:
After the bin collection this morning, all bins were left on the pavement blocking its use. Shortly after taking the picture, someone in a wheelchair had tried make their way down the road, however had to go onto the road to get past the bins and where they needed to be. This is a health and safety issue, please can it not be repeated.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Kent Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree blocking pavement, 6th May
item.description:
Green St, close to the bus stop opposite Mayfield Rd, there is a tree on the edge of Durants Park that is overgrown and hanging so low it is impossible to walk under, only a small place to walk by the curb in the road. Needs cutting.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Street, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 6th May
item.description:
Builder is constantly dumping rubbish onto the pavement and not getting a skip. No one can walk on the pavement. Rubbish and rubble is constantly being dumped onto pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fordyce Road, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Flytipped TV, wooden table left outside 26. Metal bin Flytipped by wheelie bins. All items do not belong to 26.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Devonshire Drive, Greenwich

item.title: Households rubbish and furniture on Perch Street E8, 6th May
item.description:
Hello, please can you remove the large amount of dumoed bags and furniture on Perch Street E8. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Perch Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Waste dumped, 6th May
item.description:
Lots of rubbish dumped by 64 Little Heath. Semi regular occurrence.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Little Heath, Charlton

item.title: Flytipping, 6th May
item.description:
Flytipped building rubbish

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Elder Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned lime bikes, 6th May
item.description:
Subject: Ongoing Issue with Lime Bikes Obstructing Pavements and Driveways on Revelon Road Good Morning I hope you are well. I am writing to raise an ongoing concern regarding Lime bikes being left scattered across Revelon Road on a weekly basis. The bikes are frequently abandoned on the road, on the pavement, against trees, and in some cases directly in front of parked cars. On several occasions, they have blocked access to my driveway at No. 57. It is also taking several days for these bikes to be removed which is causing repeated inconvenience and creating unnecessary hazards for pedestrians, drivers, and residents. Could you please advise what the correct procedure is for arranging the prompt collection of Lime bikes when they are left obstructing the pavement or road on Revelon Road? Additionally, I would appreciate clarification on what response times residents should expect once a report is made. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your guidance on how this issue should be handled going forward. Kind regards, Yvonne Revelon Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48-78, Revelon Road, Brockley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on street, 6th May
item.description:
Litter on street and overflowing recycling/bagged recycling.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shortlands Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: I need to report waste/faeces outside my front door that needs urgent cleaning, 6th May
item.description:
There appears to be faeces outside my front door at 113 Gibbon Road SE15 3XF. It’s directly outside my entrance and I don’t have any pets, so it hasn’t come from me. It’s a hygiene concern and I would like it removed as soon as possible. I do not have any pets, so this has not come from me. It is located right at the entrance and is a hygiene concern affecting access to my home. I would appreciate this being cleaned as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gibbon Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
